content.problemStatement:
At time 0, **R** red frogs and **G** green frogs are sitting on a straight line. Every frog can be described with an integer number **P**, denoting its initial position and a non-negative integer **V**, denoting its velocity.

Every second, a green frog jumps from its position to its left and a red frog jumps to its right. So if the initial position of a green frog is **0** and its velocity is **5**, then after **1** second, its position will be **-5**. If the color of the frog was red, then its position would be **5**. It takes negligible time for any frog to jump from its current position to the new position. Frogs are only visible on the line at any full second.

You are also given a segment **[A, B]** where **A** and **B** are two integer numbers and **−1012≤A≤B≤1012-10^{12} \leq A \leq B \leq 10^{12}−1012≤A≤B≤1012**. Please find the maximum possible number of frogs within the interesting segment at any non-negative integer second since time **0** (including 0).


Input Format
Input starts with a single positive integer, **T** (1≤T≤106)(1 \leq T \leq 10^6)(1≤T≤106), on a single line, denoting the number of test cases. Each of the following **T** test cases are represented in the following manner:



The first line has two non-negative integers **R** and **G**, 0≤R+G≤1050 \leq R+G \leq 10^50≤R+G≤105 (or 100000) on a single line.
The second line contains two integer numbers, **A** and **B** (−1012≤A≤B≤1012)(-10^{12} \leq A \leq B \leq 10^{12})(−1012≤A≤B≤1012), separated by a space. **[A, B]** is said to be the interesting segment.
Then comes **R** lines, each with two integers **P** and **V**, separated by a single space to denote the initial position and velocity of the ithi^{th}ith red frog.
Then comes **G** lines, each with two integers **P** and **V**, separated by a single space to denote the initial position and velocity of the ithi^{th}ith Green frog.


Output Format
For every case, print your output in the format, **"Case X: Y"** on a single line, where **X** is the number of the test case starting from **1** and **Y** is the maximum number of frogs that can be within the interesting segment at any point of time.
constraints: Every frog has a non-negative velocity.
The initial positions are within the range [-10^12, 10^12].
The total number of frogs in the test file doesn’t exceed 10^6.
content.inputFormat: The input starts with a single positive integer, T (1≤T≤10^6), on a single line, denoting the number of test cases. Each of the following T test cases are represented in the following manner: The first line has two non-negative integers R and G, 0≤R+G≤10^5, on a single line. The second line contains two integer numbers, A and B (-10^12≤A≤B≤10^12), separated by a space. [A, B] is said to be the interesting segment. Then comes R lines, each with two integers P and V, separated by a single space to denote the initial position and velocity of the ith red frog. Then comes G lines, each with two integers P and V, separated by a single space to denote the initial position and velocity of the ith Green frog.
content.outputFormat: For every case, print your output in the format, 'Case X: Y' on a single line, where X is the number of the test case starting from 1 and Y is the maximum number of frogs that can be within the interesting segment at any point of time.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum number of frogs within the interesting segment is 2. For the second test case, the maximum number of frogs within the interesting segment is 3.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to simulate the movement of the frogs and count the maximum number of frogs within the interesting segment at any point of time. We can use a greedy approach to find the maximum number of frogs. We will iterate over all possible time points and for each time point, we will count the number of frogs within the interesting segment.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*(R+G)*max_time) — where max_time is the maximum time point we need to consider.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(R+G) — for storing the positions and velocities of the frogs.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
# Uses input() or sys.stdin
# Handles all T test cases
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    R, G = map(int, input().split())
    A, B = map(int, input().split())
    red = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(R)]
    green = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(G)]
    max_frogs = 0
    for time in range(1000001):
        frogs = 0
        for i in range(R):
            pos = red[i][0] + red[i][1] * time
            if A <= pos <= B:
                frogs += 1
        for i in range(G):
            pos = green[i][0] - green[i][1] * time
            if A <= pos <= B:
                frogs += 1
        max_frogs = max(max_frogs, frogs)
    print(f"Case {_ + 1}: {max_frogs}")
